subject: Local agent ID mechanism — rename interactive session to ops-session

body.context: Session 91 implemented a local agent ID resolver that separates public identity (committed, transport-facing) from local operational identity (gitignored, temp files, logs). Psychology-agent now runs as 'psy-session' during interactive sessions while keeping 'psychology-agent' as the autonomous/transport identity. Operations-agent should adopt the same pattern.

what_changed.mechanism: _debug.sh (shared hook helper) resolves AGENT_ID via priority chain: env var → cached file → .claude/settings.local.json → .well-known/agent-card.json name → fallback. Cache at /tmp/{project-hash}-agent-id invalidates at session start.

instructions.summary: Add AGENT_ID env var to settings.local.json. The autonomous agent on chromabook keeps 'operations-agent' (agent-card fallback). Interactive sessions get 'ops-session'.

steps.action: Add the following at the top level of the JSON object, before permissions:

Replace the existing _debug.sh with the version from psychology-agent commit a8cec23d. Key addition: _resolve_agent_id reads AGENT_ID env var first, then settings.local.json, then agent-card.json name, then fallback. Caches result to /tmp/{project-hash}-agent-id.
steps.note: If ops hooks already source _debug.sh, this propagates automatically to all hooks.
This forces re-resolution at each session start, so changes to settings.local.json take effect immediately.

details: Chromabook autonomous sync uses agent-card fallback → 'operations-agent'

instructions.what_not_to_change: Do NOT rename the agent-card, agent-registry entries, or transport from.agent_id. The local ID serves operational concerns only: temp file namespacing (/tmp/{AGENT_ID}-*), mesh state file naming, heartbeat identification, log attribution, and multi-instance disambiguation.
instructions.future: When the autonomous operations-agent deploys on chromabook, it will use the agent-card fallback 'operations-agent'. The interactive session uses 'ops-session'. Both can run simultaneously without temp file collisions because their AGENT_ID values differ.

claims.text: The env field in settings.local.json conforms to Claude Code settings schema — validated against the schema, not a custom extension
claims.confidence_basis: Schema validation passed on psychology-agent settings.local.json

claims.text: settings.local.json remains gitignored — local identity never commits to the repo
